bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just beginning (time fraction 0.0) and I have no opponent history to analyze, I am making the opening offer. My optimal strategy is to bid my maximum possible utility to set an upper bound, which means asking for my most preferred value ('valueF') across all issues. This leaves room to concede on less important issues like issueA and issueB later as needed.

The negotiation has just begun (t=0.02), and the opponent has started with a maximal counter-offer that provides me the minimum possible utility. To signal a willingness to negotiate while maintaining a very high utility for myself, I will make a minor concession on my lowest-priority issue (issueA) decreasing from valueF to valueE, while holding firm on my high-priority issues (issueC and issueD).

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.04), but the opponent has repeated their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility. To invite them to move away from their initial position, I will make another minor concession on my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB), while holding completely firm on my highest-weight issues (issueC and issueD) to keep my overall expected utility well above 0.9.

The time is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent remains inflexible, repeating their lowest-utility demand for all issues. Since issueA and issueB are my least important issues, I will continue making small concessions there to signal a willingness to cooperate, while holding firm on my highest-priority issues, issueC and issueD, maintaining an overall utility well above 0.90.

The time is still very early (t=0.08), but the opponent has held completely firm on their initial offer of all 'valueA's. I will incrementally concede on my lowest-importance issue (issueA) down to 'valueB' to see if a near-total concession on one issue prompts them to begin moving on the issues I care about most (issueC and issueD).

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their initial offer, providing no concessions so far. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.1), I will test their flexibility by fully conceding to their requested value on my least important issue (issueA) while holding firm on the issues that are significantly more important to my utility (issueC and issueD). This offer targets a very comfortable utility of 0.888.

The negotiation is still early (t=0.12). The opponent has been stubbornly repeating the same offer for every turn ("valueA" for all issues), refusing to concede. I have already made significant concessions on my lowest-weight issues (issueA to valueA, issueB to valueE) without any reciprocal movement. I will make a small concession on issueB to 'valueD' to signal continued willingness to negotiate, but I will firmly hold my top-weighted issues (issueD and issueC) at 'valueF'.

Since it is still extremely early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I must avoid conceding too quickly without any reciprocity. However, to keep making small constructive moves, I will concede one more step on my second least important issue (issueB) down to valueC, while strictly maintaining my highest importance issues (issueC and issueD) at valueF.

The opponent is holding firm on their opening bid, which provides me with my absolute lowest utility. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will make another small concession on a lower-weight issue to demonstrate a willingness to compromise and encourage them to move. I will hold firm on my most important issues (issueC and issueD) while dropping issueB slightly to valueB.

Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I have the time to test their bottom line. I have fully conceded my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB) to their preferred values, but I am holding absolutely firm holding on my top-priority issues (issueC and issueD) at my maximal values to signal where my boundaries lie.

To show continued good faith and begin exploring trades on my more important issues, I will now slightly concede on issueC, but I must hold firm on issueD as it is my highest priority. I am maintaining a utility well above my reservation value while testing if you are willing to reciprocate.

Since we are currently at early stages (t=0.22) and the opponent is completely uncompromising, I will slow down my concessions drastically. I will maintain my high utility on these crucial issues while testing a slight variation of the lower-priority issues to see if the opponent can be prompted to compromise.
